trigger.reason: OpenAI released new voice models with simultaneous speak-and-listen capability for live conversations—a major product release from a top-tier lab with broad market attention and direct threat to customer-service software, live translation tools, and accessibility-focused SaaS.

primary_event.headline: OpenAI releases new voice models for more natural live conversations
primary_event.summary: OpenAI announced new voice models capable of speaking and listening simultaneously, enabling more natural real-time conversations and live translation. This represents a significant capability upgrade to OpenAI's voice offering and expands the potential market for voice-based AI applications.

secondary_events.headline: SpaceXAI releases Grok 4.5, which Elon describes as an 'Opus-class model'
Meta just launched a new AI generator, Muse Image, and users are already pushing back over use of their photos

discovery.trigger_acknowledgment: OpenAI released new voice models with simultaneous speak-and-listen capability for live conversations, flagging customer-service, translation, and accessibility SaaS as at-risk sectors.
discovery.run_summary: The OpenAI voice-model release triggered a broad sympathy fade across vertical SaaS names (BLKB, NCNO, PAYC, DUOL, HCAT, PCTY) even though the actual capability shipped — real-time voice conversation — has minimal overlap with donor management, bank loan origination, payroll compliance, gamified language learning, healthcare analytics, or HR SaaS. The semis names (ONTO, AMBA) moved up, not down, so they don't qualify as sympathy-fade victims. The cleanest overdone cases are the vertical-workflow SaaS names where regulatory/compliance moats are explicit in the Risk Factors and the voice capability isn't the vector of disruption.

discoveries.name: Blackbaud, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors flag AI as a general competitive/market-perception concern ('Market perception of the role and impact of AI on technology companies may adversely affect our stock price, regardless of our actual operating performance') but the core business is nonprofit/fundraising/donor management software with 3-year subscription terms, embedded payment processing, and a Security Incident-driven regulatory moat. No voice-AI exposure identified.
discoveries.thesis: OpenAI's voice model release has no plausible mechanism to displace donor-management workflows, integrated payments, or peer-to-peer fundraising platforms. Blackbaud's own Risk Factors explicitly warn that stock price may decline on AI market perception 'regardless of our actual operating performance' — this is exactly the pattern we're seeing today.
discoveries.what_confirms: Bounce-back over the next 5-10 sessions absent a Blackbaud-specific negative catalyst; volume today was below average (0.58x), suggesting a low-conviction retail-driven fade.
discoveries.what_kills: A subsequent 8-K disclosing customer churn, a security incident update, or renewal-cohort weakness in the ~40% of contracts up for renewal in 2026.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Vertical nonprofit SaaS names sell off on generic AI headlines even when the shipped capability (voice) has zero product overlap — the Risk Factor language explicitly anticipates this dynamic.
discoveries.catalyst: OpenAI releases new voice models for more natural live conversations
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: BLKB is in the AI-adjacent SaaS basket and dropped materially on a day dominated by the OpenAI voice announcement, with no company-specific catalyst signals.
research_pointers: Check whether any 8-K filings or press releases hit today or in the prior 48 hours
Review 2026 renewal cohort commentary in most recent earnings call transcript
Compare BLKB's move to peer nonprofit/vertical SaaS names not in this basket

discoveries.name: nCino, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors extensively discuss AI/ML as both an opportunity and competitive risk, but the customer base is highly regulated FIs with 3-5 year contracts, deeply embedded in core banking systems, subject to OCC/FDIC examination, and built on Salesforce Platform. No voice-AI-specific exposure — the AI risks discussed are about LLM-driven loan origination competition, not real-time conversational voice.
discoveries.thesis: OpenAI's voice model release does not threaten bank loan origination or onboarding workflows — these require regulatory audit trails, KYC/AML compliance, and integration with core banking systems that a voice agent cannot replicate. Move is thematic AI-SaaS aversion, not substantive.
discoveries.what_confirms: Recovery in the following sessions absent a bank-vertical negative datapoint; the five-day change is still positive, suggesting this was a one-day sympathy dip.
discoveries.what_kills: Evidence that a major FI customer is piloting a competing LLM-based loan origination platform, or a downgrade tied to AI displacement risk.
discoveries.catalyst: OpenAI releases new voice models for more natural live conversations
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: NCNO is a workflow SaaS name that trades in sympathy with generic AI-displacement narratives despite the voice capability being irrelevant to bank back-office workflows.
research_pointers: Confirm no downgrade or preannouncement 8-K today
Check whether Salesforce dependency risk has been amplified by any recent Salesforce commentary
Review AI adoption commentary from most recent nCino earnings call for customer receptivity signals

discoveries.name: Paycom Software, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors discuss AI as a disruptive technology risk and note Paycom has licensed a third-party LLM for internal AI tools, but the core business is HCM/payroll — payroll tax filing, ACH transfers, jurisdictional employment law compliance. No voice-AI product overlap; competitors listed are ADP/Paychex/Paylocity, not conversational AI vendors.
discoveries.thesis: Voice models don't file 941s, run payroll ACH, or handle multi-state UI compliance. Paycom's moat is regulatory infrastructure and being the bank-of-record for employee comp — a text/voice interface doesn't displace that. Five-day change is still strongly positive, so this is a single-day sympathy dip.
discoveries.what_confirms: Rebound absent a company-specific catalyst; volume today was very light (0.47x), suggesting low conviction.
discoveries.what_kills: An unusual client-loss disclosure or evidence that agentic AI is being adopted for payroll compliance in a regulated context.
discoveries.what_to_learn: HR/payroll SaaS with heavy regulatory compliance moats are consistent false-victims of AI-displacement narratives — track PAYC/PCTY/PAYX co-movement on future AI trigger days.
discoveries.catalyst: OpenAI releases new voice models for more natural live conversations
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: PAYC is on the AI-adjacent HR/payroll SaaS list and dropped in sympathy with a generic AI capability release that has no payroll-workflow application.
research_pointers: Compare PAYC move to PCTY, ADP, PAYX for co-movement pattern
Check institutional flow — light volume suggests retail-driven

discoveries.name: Paylocity Holding Corporation
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors flag AI as a competitive risk ('New technologies that involve AI or machine learning ... may emerge that are able to deliver solutions at lower prices') but the core business rests on payroll tax filing, ACH/wire banking relationships, and multi-jurisdictional compliance. Voice AI is not the disruption vector — the risk section discusses cybersecurity implications of AI more than product-replacement risk.
discoveries.thesis: Same thesis as PAYC — voice models don't handle payroll tax filings, ACH banking relationships, or state-by-state employment law compliance. The five-day change is still strongly positive, confirming this is a one-day thematic dip on the OpenAI headline.
discoveries.what_confirms: Rebound in the following sessions; PCTY and PAYC co-moving on the downside supports the sympathy-fade read rather than name-specific news.
discoveries.what_kills: A negative preannouncement or evidence that agentic AI is displacing HR SaaS in Paylocity's SMB/mid-market segment.
discoveries.catalyst: OpenAI releases new voice models for more natural live conversations
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: PCTY is in the HR/payroll SaaS blast radius of generic AI displacement narratives despite voice being product-irrelevant.
research_pointers: Track PCTY/PAYC correlation over next 3-5 days
Review most recent guidance for AI-impact commentary

discoveries.name: Duolingo, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors explicitly acknowledge generative AI as a competitive risk ('It is possible that a new product could gain rapid scale at the expense of existing brands through harnessing a new technology (such as GenAI)') and note that improved translation services could reduce interest in language learning. However, Duolingo has its own AI-powered tier (Duolingo Max) and the moat is the daily habit loop / gamification, not instructional content quality.
discoveries.thesis: OpenAI's real-time voice conversation capability is more directly relevant to Duolingo than to most names in this basket — but Duolingo already offers AI-powered voice conversation practice (Duolingo Max), and the core moat is the streak/gamification dopamine loop, not instructional depth. The five-day change is still strongly positive, and today's move is a modest thematic dip, not a real repricing.
discoveries.what_confirms: Duolingo continues to announce voice features that leverage (not compete with) frontier voice models; five-day trend remains positive.
discoveries.what_kills: MAU/DAU deceleration data or evidence that Duolingo Max subscriber growth is stalling as users shift to native ChatGPT voice for language practice — this would elevate to 'partial' or 'justified'.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Consumer edtech sells off on every consumer AI announcement even when the target has already integrated the underlying tech — worth tracking whether the reflex fades over time as investors get used to Duolingo's AI-native positioning.
discoveries.catalyst: OpenAI releases new voice models for more natural live conversations
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Language learning is arguably the most emotionally salient consumer use case for voice AI, so DUOL is in the direct blast radius of any voice-model release — the question is whether the panic is warranted (it isn't, based on filings and product positioning).
research_pointers: Check Duolingo Max attach rate and voice-feature adoption commentary from latest earnings
Monitor DAU trends via app-store rank data
Look for Duolingo response/product announcement leveraging the new OpenAI voice models

discoveries.name: Health Catalyst, Inc
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors describe intense competition from EHR vendors and analytics competitors but the AI risk is generic — no voice-AI-specific exposure. Business is provider data warehousing with multi-year subscription contracts and deep EHR integration (HIPAA/BAA required).
discoveries.thesis: Voice AI has no plausible application to healthcare provider analytics/data warehousing. However, HCAT has real company-specific issues (Project Nexus restructuring, CEO transition, DOS-to-Ignite migration churn) that make it hard to isolate the sympathy-fade signal from operational headwinds. Modest confidence and small position size warranted.
discoveries.what_confirms: Rebound in coming sessions with no name-specific news; five-day change is still positive.
discoveries.what_kills: Any incremental negative on Project Nexus execution, DOS migration churn, or a downgrade — HCAT has real issues that could dominate the tape independent of AI sentiment.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Small-cap healthcare IT names with existing operational fragility are unreliable sympathy-fade plays — the AI thesis can be right but company-specific overhangs dominate.
discoveries.catalyst: OpenAI releases new voice models for more natural live conversations
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: HCAT is in the AI-adjacent healthcare-analytics basket and dropped modestly on an AI-heavy news day.
research_pointers: Assess Project Nexus execution risk before initiating any position
Review most recent guidance versus consensus
Check short interest and small-cap technical setup — $2 stock, $162M cap = high volatility

skipped.reason: Positive move today (up on the day); not a sympathy-fade victim.
